desc:
The forest has thinned here, allowing tall grasses to invade the forest. An
occasional shrub can be found, ready and willing to block your way, or provide a
few vines full of sharp thorns to scratch at your flesh.
name: Dreary Forest

desc:
The thinning forest here has led to a greater amount of underbrush. To the north
the growth is so thick it seems to squeeze the life from the trees, while to the
south the forest has fallen to disease.
name: Dreary Forest

desc:
The forest opens to the south, to the scene of a broken shack. In all other
directions the forest thickens, much to thick to be passable to north, but still
thin enough to travel east or west. One noticable aspect of this area is all the
uprooted trees, and those trees lacking branches.
name: Dreary Forest

desc:
They say that if you don't ask, the trees won't tell you. Here, it might be
preferable if you do not ask, for the small animals stuck through like rotting
shish kebabs on the tree limbs give off an odor bad enough to make you sick to
your stomach already.
name: Dark Dreary Forest

desc:
Within the heart of the forest things just seem gloomier. No normal forest
sounds can be heard, perhaps it is just your passing, but the small animals that
live here must have become accustomed to danger. Only the silent will survive.
name: Dark Dreary Forest

desc:
The earth here has been mauled on an almost continous basis. Or, perhaps it
might be more accurate to say, nightly. It seems as if something has been eating
the roots of the trees. Certainly something with a voracious appetite.
name: Dark Dreary Forest

desc:
The midst of the forest bespeaks ages of sorrow. Bent limbs seem to reach out to
you, begging for your ability to move to more hospitable territory. Even the
roots seem to reach out from the earth, eager to get away from whatever lurks
below.
name: Dark Dreary Forest

desc:
The trees here hang their limbs low, seeming to hold the weight of Arda upon
their broken and bent wood. You ask yourself what could be worse, to be a tree,
forced to endure the sights and sounds within this grisly forest, or to be one
of the victims.
name: Dark Dreary Forest

desc:
The underbrush has grown thick here, effectively blocking passage through the
forest to the south and west. Many brambles and vines seem to have made a nice
home for themselves here, tearing at your flesh as if they survived on your
blood and not the sunshine they get so little of.
name: Dark Dreary Forest

desc:
Sorrow speaks loudly its name within these woods. No stars and no sun can be
seen through the thick canopy, only the cool air as it rustles the branches
above. No doubt because of the thick canopy overhead, the forest floor has very
little brush, and can be navigated easily, with the exception a large stand of
trees to the south that are thick enough to block travel southwards.
name: Dark Dreary Forest

desc:
The stand of trees has grown too thick to be passable to the south, but
elsewhere the thick overhead canopy has prevented the extensive growth of
underbrush that clogs the passage south.
name: Dark Dreary Forest

desc:
Several large holes can be seen in the canopy overhead, letting through light
from the sky above. During the daytime the nastiest beasts that inhabit the
forest will surely be kept at bay, but at night this world belongs to different
owners.
name: Dreary Forest

desc:
Like many pillars reaching up to support a vast ceiling of green, the forest
gives you the feel of a vast indoor arena. An arena that only the worms and
fungi clean, and the fun begins at night. Prepare to be the next gladiator.
name: Dreary Forest

desc:
Overhead the forest is thick, down below it is barren. The smell is unique
however, a combination of evergreen, musty wet air, and odor of a rotting
corpse. Probably the deer corpse up ahead, with the fungi sprouting around it.
name: Dark Dreary Forest

desc:
The forest darkens the horizon in all directions except north, where you can see
a barren area filled with boulders. You can hear the wind whistling a tune of
sorrow around the trees, begging for the future to bring happier times.
name: Dreary Forest

desc:
A thick canopy blocks the sun from reaching the forest floor in this area.
Mushrooms can be seen clinging to the trees, and a foul stench arrises from
their rotting pussing caps. Probably wise to avoid mushrooms that thrive in
decay.
name: Dark Dreary Forest

desc:
Horrid smells overtake you, and you notice the rotting remains of something at
the base of a tree. Whatever it was, something took great joy in smashing it's
skull against the trunk of a tree. Perhaps it is best to leave the trees to
their fate and escape with your own life intact.
name: Dark Dreary Forest

desc:
Thick trees, bent trees, all of which seem to reach to the sky for some saviour.
Although their foliage is thick enough to keep the yellow eye at bay, this
hasn't helped their roots and trunks, as evidenced by many scratches and gashes
in their bark.
name: Dark Dreary Forest

desc:
The forest can be seen thinning to the north, finally giving way to a field
farther north. Eastwards a rocky slope stops the forest from spreading its
depression any farther east. A few of the trees at the edge seem ready to fall
down the rocks, their roots straining to hold them up.
name: Dreary Forest

desc:
The edge of the forest, with its forlorn and bent trees has reached its end
here. As the trees thin, the underbrush grows thick, and its thorny appendages
constantly snag you, as if trying to drag you back. An occasional fungus can be
seen pushing its bulbous top through the earth.
name: Dreary Forest

desc:
All around this small clearing the trees and brush seem to reach towards you,
anxious to cover more land. The land seems to have been trampled often by large
beasts. What few plants remain have been crushed numerous times.
name: Clearing

desc:
The forest has provided for its own here. There are many nice places to hide
behind the bushes while keeping an eye on the clearing to the south. From the
flattened bushes you can see that someone has been using this area recently.
name: Dreary Forest

desc:
The smell of putrid rot hangs in the air, as if someplace, hidden beneath the
roots of a tree, may lie the corpse of an unlucky traveller. The woods have
never been hospitable to humans or hobbits, and it has only grown worse with the
passing of each day.
name: Dark Dreary Forest

desc:
The fertile soil of the forest gives way to the rocky ground of a gully to the
west. Its steep sides look passable here, but certainly not as easy as the woods
in all other directions.
name: Dreary Forest

desc:
As if a bone was ripped from the earth here, this gully bares the rocks that
make up the foundation of the land. Starting west of here a short ways, the
gully bends to the north where it's sides are steeper and the rocks seem to have
sharper points.
name: Gully

desc:
This rocky terrain seems to funnel to the east, making moving in other
directions possible albeit dangerous. Here and there can be seen an occasional
bone, leftovers from someone's dinner. Someone very big.
name: Rough Ground

desc:
Grasses grow tall in this strange clearing. The trees seem to have avoided this
area, their branches actually turned away, leaving naked trunks exposed to
whatever menace might lurk unseen here.
name: Glade

desc:
The woods here have been twisted horribly, as if the evil of the east had shaped
them to his desires, then froze them in that state to prevent any feeling but
agony to enter their souls.
name: Warped Woods

desc:
The ravine heads north and south here, with forest high up to the east and west.
The walls to the east and west, too steep to climb, seem perfect for the few
vultures that sit in the trees high overhead, watching for a spare morsel. The
crack in the earth deepens to the north.
name: Narrow Ravine

desc:
Nefarious grins seem to be traced in the bark of the trees here, their branches
reaching out as if to drag you into some hidden maw. Even their roots can be
seen to wiggle, as if blowing gently in the wind.
name: Warped Woods

desc:
These ghastly woods seem ready to charge you, each with a sinister face painted
in the lines of it's bark, always looking, always watching you. Some short
trees, almost midgets compared to their cousins can be seen to have roots lying
mostly on top of the soil.
name: Warped Woods

desc:
The trees here seem to move and bend, forcing you along a path of their
choosing. Their trunks twisted, their leaves sprouting from bark, it would seem
Morgoth himself had paid a visit to this place.
name: Dark Warped Woods

desc:
The trees here bend as if they were dancing, frozen in impossible positions.
Some stand with trunks arched as if doing somersaults, others bend as if they
were a vine and not a tree.
name: Warped Woods

desc:
Branches delve into the ground here, only to spring forth with leaves a few feet
away. Madness seems to have reached its peak here, with what seems to be roots
climbing the trunks of other trees as if they were vines.
name: Dark Warped Woods

desc:
While this may not be the most hospitable place around, the rocky walls of this
chasm seem to offer some protection from the evil forces that warp the forest
above. Of course, that may all be just an illusion.
name: Rocky Ravine

desc:
The ravine curves again here, leading to the north and west. The walls to the
east and south may be climbable, and you can see the forest towering overhead.
One can almost hear a faint thumping noise coming from below.
name: Crook in the Ravine

desc:
The crevice bends again here, heading to the east and south. The walls here are
not so steep as those to the south, and may be climbable, although the woods
overlooking the crevice urge you to reconsider that direction.
name: Crook in the Ravine

desc:
The forest is sparse here, a few trees looking over a ravine to the west, and
thick underbrush preventing movement anywhere but back south. This area seems a
bit less weary than the rest of the forest, perhaps because some light actually
warms the earth in places here.
name: Forested Edge

desc:
The weary forest looks out over a bend in a ravine situated to the west. North
and south the forest continues to be traversable, but the easterly direction has
been blocked by trees that seem to have been planted close together on purpose.
name: Dreary Forest

desc:
No light reaches the forest floor here, the canopy above providing enough
nourishment for the large trees to sustain them against the withering trials
given to the by the evil beasts that roam the forest. It is said that trolls
turn to stone at the touch of a ray of sun, and perhaps it is the trolls
knowledge of this that keeps these trees unharmed in this dangerous area.
name: Dark Dreary Forest

desc:
The forest stops abruptly to the north, where a steep slope leads into a wide
chasm. Going north may be unwise for those who wish to stay in the shelter of
the forest. Not that these woods bring peace of mind, but at least any eyes that
may be watching you must be skillful in keeping quiet as well.
name: Dreary Forest

desc:
Westwards the underbrush has covered the forest floor making It impassable, and
north the earthen ground gives way to large rocks and a steep slope. An entire
body has been nailed to the trunk of a tree near the rocky slope - it appears to
have been an unlucky dwarf and its feet dangle only inches from the ground.
name: Dreary Forest

desc:
Something has made this part of the forest its very own trophy room. The heads
of just about anything, from stags to elves and orcs, can be seen impaled on the
branches here. It is odd that the heads have all been placed only 4 to 5 feet
off the ground.
name: Dark Dreary Forest

desc:
Grasses and small bushes have taken this clearing for their own, fending off the
forest that has encroached on them from all directions but south. The darkness
hiding behind the trees can almost be felt, trying to press towards this
clearing, only to be stopped by the open sky above.
name: Opening in the Forest

desc:
The opening in the forest here reminds you of a valley, with the grasses and
bushes looking so small against the backdrop of large trees that guard their
flanks. If fenced off this area would surely make a good corral or pasture.
name: Opening in the Forest

desc:
The tall grasses that populate this field seemed to have overgrown themselves
without any farm animals to feed on them. The weeds have been the winners in
this competition, leaving the weaker plants to shrivel and die.
name: Withering Grass

desc:
This rocky slope descends to the east, winding away from the forest to the
south. It would almost seem that someone has purposefully placed the rocks here
in unsteady positions, for even the largest rocks tip and move as you walk on
them.
name: Rocky Slope

desc:
You have arrived at the base of a low cliff. Its walls much too steep for
climbing, it appears to be used as a camp quite frequently. A small campfire can
be seen, but it is currently extinguished.
name: Rocky End

desc:
The tower of stone comes to an end in an area with very low walls, only about
half a foot high and no ceiling. Wind and rain have left their marks on the once
firm floor, made of thick wooden planks. Above, the open sky looks to goes on
forever. Looking outward the surrounding landscape can be seen for a very far
distance. Undulating hills and a circling trail can be seen to the south.
name: Top of the Tower

desc:
From here you have a better view to what appears to be a tall tower on the edge
of the forest. It rises just east of here and goes up maybe ten yeards before
reaching the top. In the west and north the canopy continues, but seems to be
less accessible than here, unless you are a bird.
name: Up in the Canopy

desc:
Misshapen trees give way to a rocky gully to the north. The wind can be heard
sweeping across the trees and through the gully, making a howling noise, a
perpetual scream quite suitable to the atmosphere of the rest of the forest.
name: Dreary Forest

desc:
A ravine opens up towards the south. The hollow in the northern wall of the
ravine provides a stony roof above, offering some protection from the elements.
This grotto could possibly mark the beginning of a deeper cave, as a huge bear
fur hung against the inner wall seems to cover an entrance deeper into the
underground.
name: Rocky Shelter

desc:
While this was in all likelihood a natural cavern in the past, it has been
expanded with crude tools to form a wide, high-ceilinged corridor. On the roof
above, broken remnants of stalactites cling to the rock. Anything reaching down
below ten feet has been hewn off, leaving jagged, shattered remains. On the
lightly grey limestone floor, any stalagmites rising up to meet the opposing
stalactites have been removed with brute force. The now widened corridor leads
deeper into a cavern to the west, while a huge bear fur has been hung on the
southern wall to cover what looks like a narrowing tunnel.
name: Limestone Corridor

desc:
These caves may well be natural in origin but have been converted into a rustic
living area by whomever that now occupy them. Rocks or boulders one would expect
in a cave have been cleared out, and the ground is worn smooth by the regular
passage of its inhabitants. Towards the west, the cave leads through a narrow
opening from which scents of meat and blood come drifting on the slowly
circulating air.
name: Wide Diverging Tunnel

desc:
Four tunnels lead off in different directions from this subterranean juncture.
The cavern is lofty, the ceiling no less than ten feet high at its lowest point.
A vague scent of wood smoke fills the air, hinting at a recent fire in one of
the nearby caves.
name: The Juncture

desc:
This wide tunnel takes a rough, uneven path through the surrounding rock, moving
through this area in a south-to-west bend. A small spring seeps up from the
cavern floor at the eastern wall, gathering in a small pool to provide fresh
water. The dust on the ground is mostly cleared away in a path from the pool and
towards the south; although more because of regular traffic than any obvious
intent to keep the area neat and clean. The tunnel to the west seem a lot less
disturbed.
name: Wide Tunnel by a Spring

desc:
This narrow cave has been converted into a crude larder. The walls are speckled
with spots of dried blood, and on the dusty floor dark, drying stains attract
flies and other insects. Simple iron hooks have been set into the walls, ready
for whatever meats may be hung from them.
name: Stone Giant's Larder

desc:
A low, narrow opening to the west seem to be the only entrance to this dwelling.
The interior is stark, the walls harsh bare stone and the floor rough and
uneven. Three tall piles of grass, leaves and animal furs could be passed off as
sleeping places, and the scorched stones in the centre of the chamber suggest
that at times a fire is built here.
name: Stark Dwelling

desc:
A very large set of stairs has been hewn out of the hard rock, leading further
down into deep caverns. The cavern floor has been cleared of irregularities and
any sharp or protruding stone has been removed from the walls. With a little
decoration and lighting, this cave could almost pass as a hallway.
name: Top of Stone Stairs

desc:
The tunnel enters this wide, spacious grotto from the east. To the south and
west, hard solid walls of rock block any passage, but towards the north the cave
opens up and the far wall is barely discernible. Travel northwards is
interrupted by a jagged crevice, and the only obvious way onwards in that
direction is to climb down into the crevice and find a way from there.
name: Shelf above a Crevice

desc:
This crevice is narrow, with steep and jagged walls and sharp rocks everywhere.
At the very southwestern end of the crevice, a rock shelf is visible. This looks
to be the only reasonable place to climb out of here. If one were inclined to
stay in the crevice, it appears to continue eastwards.
name: Underground Crevice

desc:
Huge steps have been hewn out of the solid rock to the west, where a slight
slope ascends and provides easier passage to the levels above. Each step is so
high that a hobbit would need to use his hands to climb to the next, and so wide
that said hobbit could place his bed on it. The cavern floor looks well trodden,
free of loose rocks and remarkably smooth.
name: Foot of Stone Stairs

desc:
The cavern system comes to a dead end here, and this small cave has been
converted to a crude abode. Judging from the size of the long, wooden bunks
against the back wall, the inhabitants are of impressive dimensions. The walls
are rough but seem to have been smoothened to some extent, the worst of the
sharp edges chopped away.
name: Crude Abode

desc:
This deep cleft is barely passable. The broken, jagged rocks of the crevice's
walls makes travel to the east and west very difficult. Climbing out of the deep
fissure is completely out of the question, the sides being much too steep, the
rock cracked and broken; and most likely prone to falling apart if any weight is
put upon it. The ground is littered with rubble fallen from the walls.
name: Deep in an Underground Crevice

desc:
Here at the eastern end of this crevice, deep beneath the surface, little hope
is given to a weary adventurer. The bottom of this crevice is a bleak place, the
ground littered with broken rocks and fallen rubble. The sides of the deep
fissure appear impossible to climb and the only viable direction of travel is to
follow the course of the crevice towards the west.
name: Eastern End of an Underground Crevice

desc:
Here, the vast cavern that extends west of here ends in a small retreat. Lots of
rubble lies strewn across the cavern floor, fallen from the unstable roof above.
Most of the cavern walls and gravel consists of hard, grey stone prone to
breaking apart into smaller shards with sharp, jagged edges.
name: Rubble-strewn Cavern

desc:
The size of this wide, vast cavern would put the throne room in many a castle to
shame. The walls stand far enough apart to be shrouded in inky gloom. A great
aggregation of rocky formations on the floor level makes the area hard to
navigate. The floor itself is full of cracks, some of which are wide enough to
step into if one were careless.
name: Vast Natural Cavern

desc:
A cave-in has created an enormous mass of crushed boulders along a wall in this
colossal cavern. Any pathway in that direction has been lost forever. The
remainder of the cave can still be described as pasture-sized. The rock is of a
dark grey variety, and the rugged formations of the walls, floor and ceiling
leaves no doubt that this cave is a work of nature.
name: Vast Natural Cavern

desc:
Faint sounds of dripping water can be heard echoing from some dark corner of
this gigantic grotto. A wide variety of rocks make up the walls and floor, much
of it granite but also volcanic rock types. Pillars of basalt stand tall in the
stale air, remnants of chutes of volcanic magma that solidified in ages long
gone. There is also thin veins of pitch black obsidian embedded in the rock bed,
the slivers of glass-like rock glitter alluringly.
name: Deep in a Vast Cavern

desc:
A vast, natural cavern has formed here, deep down underground. The ground is
very uneven, with cliff-like formations several yards high that rise from the
floor throughout the cave. Much of the rock seem rich in ore, and there are
veins of a dark, rusty red colour and some of brighter yellow shades.
name: Deep in a Vast Cavern

desc:
This smaller tunnel connects two huge, open caverns to the east and west, while
to the north and south the walls of rough rock stand much closer together. The
ground falls away in a steep treacherous fall to the east. A heedless traveller
could walk right off the edge and crash down into the dark depths of this
subterranean world.
name: Edge of a Steep Cliff

desc:
The ground here is covered in thick layers of knee-high bat guano. The
cave-dwelling bats roost in the airy lofts high above, where they must have
found access to the outside world. The air is filled with a stinging, acrid
scent. The deep aggregation of guano continues uninterrupted towards the east,
while the cave southwards may be a route to cleaner places.
name: Guano Deluge

desc:
Vast deposits of bat guano have accrued on the floor of this grotto. The white,
acidic droppings lie in layers thick enough to reach a hobbit's waist, filling
the air with acrid, unpleasant smells. The cavern continues to the east and
west, and so too does the guano, although there seems to be slightly less of it
towards the east.
name: Guano Deluge

desc:
The fluttering sounds of batwings higher up in the lofty spaces of this cavern
is ever present. A shaft can be glimpsed high above, likely a route the
cavern-dwelling bats take to the surface. Down here at the cavern floor, a great
deal of spider webs have been spun between boulders and ledges. The webs reach
as far up as halfway to the shaft above. Higher up, where the walls are closer
together, the great webs stretch from wall to wall, obviously intended to
entangle any bat that swoops down too low.
name: Hunting Grounds

desc:
The sides of this steep but cragged hole are lit by a faint sickly green glow
from patches of phosphorescent moss growing on the cave walls. A tunnel opens up
to the west, the opening partly covered by strands of sticky spider webs. Above,
the edge of the hole is easily reachable even for those of humble stature.
name: Deep Hole

desc:
Faint skittering sounds can be heard from the north and west. Noise travels far
in these deep tunnels and the sounds are faint enough to perhaps be imagined. A
snarl of webbings line the cave, covering boulders and deep dark holes in the
uneven walls.
name: Threshold of a Spider Nest

desc:
The ceiling hangs down low enough in this grotto to give even the least
claustrophobic of travellers pause. The cavern is made of a mineral-rich rock
mostly a light grey in colour. Only the lack of seeping water keeps this cave
traversible, for if the stunted stalactites hanging down from the ceiling were
of the lengths such formations can grow into, they would meet the floor and
close this tunnel completely. Passageways leads off to the east, west, and
north.
name: Diverging Tunnels

desc:
This dark tunnel, far beneath the fresh air and open spaces of the outside
world, show plenty of signs of a habitual presence of spiders. Webs have been
spun, stretching between rocks and boulders, crags and stalactites. Any careless
movement could cause severe entanglement in the sticky traps.
name: Webbed Tunnel

desc:
A great number of stalagmites have formed across the expanse of this cavern,
rising up from the floor as thick, peaked pillars. Above hang the corresponding
stalactites, which are much slimmer in shape. Many spider webs have been spun
here: most of them are empty, although some of them hold the dried husks of bats
or lizards.
name: Webbed Tunnel

desc:
Here, deep down near the roots of the world, the rocks enclosing this narrow
twisting passageway are as black as the darkest night: shot through with veins
of faintly glittering minerals. A fair amount of spider webs cover the walls,
some of it new and sticky and some merely tangles of old, dry cobwebs.
name: Dark Corner

desc:
The presence of spiders is evident no matter in which direction one turns.
Thick, sticky webs cover the walls, floor and ceiling and the dry, empty husks
of past prey litters the ground. A smaller chamber lies to the north, the
entrance so covered in webs it could easily be overlooked. Towards the south
there seem to be less webs, and more of the dark rock beneath visible. A way out
would surely lie in that direction.
name: Deep in the Spider's Nest

desc:
This entire grotto functions as a hatchery for the spiders of the area. Grey,
bulging eggsacks hang from sticky spider webs and tiny, hatched eggs litter the
floor. Most of the cave is covered in thick webs, the strands far too thick to
having been spun by a common house spider.
name: The Hatchery

desc:
Great webs spun by what must be truly intimidating spiders cover holes and
cavities in the walls, possibly providing nests and hiding places. A patch of
bare rock on the southern wall is surprisingly free of webs, and the stone is a
much lighter grey than the surrounding stone. The tunnel leads towards the east,
where faint reflections of water can be glimpsed.
name: Webbed Tunnel

desc:
Tremendous patience and effort has gone into entangling the walls, floor and
ceiling of this lair in a mesh of webs. Here and there, dry old husks of bats
and lizards can be seen among the cobwebs, leftovers from creatures the spiders
in these deep caves have feasted upon.
name: Spider's Lair

desc:
A dark pool has formed in a hollow in the eastern part of this cave. Thin,
pointed stalactites hang above the pool, some so long that their tips touch the
surface of the water. Drops of mineral-enriched water fall from the smaller
stalactites, as if they were a part of a slow, steady rain.
name: Pool in the Nest

desc:
You have no idea what you have climbed into. The tree has been eaten, is being
eaten, by hordes of small worms. Each excretes a thin weblike strand behind it.
The smell the creatures make is horrid, as if some airborne toxin was setting
off alarms in your nose.
name: Top of a Webbed Tree
